steps.title: Identify the condition for y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is where the graph intersects the y-axis. At any point on the y-axis, the x-coordinate equals 0. Therefore, to find the y-intercept, we substitute $x = 0$ into the given equation.

steps.title: Substitute and evaluate
steps.content:
Substituting $x = 0$ into $y = x^2 + 27$:
$$y = 0^2 + 27$$
$$y = 0 + 27 = 27$$
The y-intercept occurs at the point $(0, 27)$, so the value of $y$ is 27.
